title: Project initialization and scoping
context: Scoped the pokemon_professor game project through dialogue and initialized all project infrastructure including the charter, deliverables scaffold, and Beads issue tracker.

decisions.title: Project named pokemon_professor
decisions.description: The project will be called pokemon_professor throughout all artifacts and tooling.
decisions.rationale: User-specified name chosen at init time.

decisions.title: Browser-based standalone platform
decisions.description: The game will run in a web browser with no server required, built with HTML/CSS/JavaScript.
decisions.rationale: Simplest delivery target for a proof-of-concept game.

decisions.title: Core game concept locked
decisions.description: Student navigates an outdoor university campus and battles 6 professors in a fixed sequence to pass their classes. Each professor is a caricature of their research field.
decisions.rationale: Defined through scoping dialogue; user confirmed the concept before init.

decisions.title: Retro pixel art visual style
decisions.description: All visuals will use simple pixel art in the style of classic 1990s–2000s Pokemon games.
decisions.rationale: User preference; aligns with the Pokemon-inspired format.

decisions.title: Turn-based battle system faithful to Pokemon
decisions.description: Battles are turn-based with moves, attacks, and a run option. The player fights solo. Win = class passed; lose = faint and restart from the beginning.
decisions.rationale: User specified faithfulness to classic Pokemon mechanics.

decisions.title: Proof-of-concept scope target
decisions.description: The initial deliverable is a proof-of-concept with one fully playable end-to-end loop, not a complete game.
decisions.rationale: Keeps scope manageable; user explicitly chose PoC over full prototype.

decisions.title: Explicit scope exclusions
decisions.description: No building interiors, no multiplayer, no save/load, no audio, no catching mechanics.
decisions.rationale: Agreed during scoping to keep the PoC focused.

title: Scope expansion and artifact schema definition
context: Expanded project scope to include simplicity/readability constraints, dialogue, multi-region maps, music, and weather. Researched vanilla JS game architecture and established the five-module structure and GDD/TDD artifact schemas.

decisions.title: Add readability and simplicity constraints to scope
decisions.description: Flat code structure, inline comments, library vetting required, no build tools. ES modules excluded due to file:// protocol limitation.
decisions.rationale: Owner wants to understand all pieces of the codebase, not just observe it being built.

decisions.title: Expand scope: dialogue, multi-region maps, music, weather
decisions.description: Added dialogue system, building interiors and hidden areas, scene-appropriate background music, and overworld weather animations. Removed prior exclusions for these features.
decisions.rationale: User confirmed all four features are desired for the PoC.

decisions.title: Adopt five-module vanilla JS architecture
decisions.description: data.js, map.js, player.js, battle.js, main.js loaded via script tags. Single central gameState object in main.js. Plain objects for game entities; no class hierarchies.
decisions.rationale: Consistent with real-world projects at this scale; matches readability constraints; no build step required.

decisions.title: Define TDD as per-module files and create GDD as single document
decisions.description: Technical design split into individual tdd/[module].md files. Game design captured in a single game_design.md. Schemas created for both artifact types before authoring begins.
decisions.rationale: Per-module TDD keeps context loading focused; GDD is a single coherent player-facing design document.

title: Game design, technical architecture, and project structure
context: Authored the full GDD and all seven TDD files, finalised the professor lineup and campus map, established the seven-module vanilla JS architecture, added portfolio framing as a project objective, and defined deliverables and success criteria.

decisions.title: Prohibit automated git push during EOS
decisions.description: Removed mandatory git push from Session Completion in CLAUDE.md and added a deny rule for git push in settings.local.json.
decisions.rationale: Pushing to GitHub should be a deliberate, user-initiated action — not an automated step that could fire without review.

decisions.title: Professor lineup and encounter order finalised
decisions.description: Six professors in order: Phonetics (pond), Syntax (classroom), Semantics (library mezzanine), DSA (PhD dungeon), NLP/Deep Learning (GPU lab), Computational Semantics (castle — final boss).
decisions.rationale: Phonetics first sets a warm, sensory tone; Computational Semantics as final boss synthesises all prior subfields and suits the castle setting.

decisions.title: Rendering strategy: Option A — distributed draw functions
decisions.description: Each module exposes a draw(ctx) function; game.js calls them in layer order each frame. No centralised renderer module.
decisions.rationale: Keeps game.js readable as a linear orchestrator — important for the flat, explicit code constraint and for portfolio legibility.

decisions.title: Seven-module architecture with data.js as standalone
decisions.description: Modules: game, engine, data, map, battle, dialogue, audio. data.js is a dedicated static data module; game.js is the entry point and wire-up layer.
decisions.rationale: Separating static data from logic keeps all modules lean and prevents data definitions from being scattered across the codebase.

decisions.title: Portfolio framing added as a project objective
decisions.description: Portfolio Objective section added to project charter. DESIGN.md added as DEL-004. bd remember entry saved.
decisions.rationale: The project serves dual purpose: a playable game and a portfolio artefact demonstrating AI-assisted methodology. Design documents are first-class deliverables.

decisions.title: VC-10 and VC-11 added to success criteria
decisions.description: VC-10 tests the castle gate mechanic (blocked until five professors defeated). VC-11 tests that DESIGN.md is legible to an outside reader.
decisions.rationale: Both scenarios were uncovered by the expanded scope and portfolio objective — neither was covered by VC-01 through VC-09.

title: Professor naming, player moves, and GDD restructure
context: Finalised the full professor roster with punny portmanteau names, expanded the player move list to six moves, and split the monolithic GDD into a gdd/ folder mirroring the tdd/ structure.

decisions.title: Professor roster finalised — 7 professors
decisions.description: Schwaartz (Phonetics), Syntaxa (Syntax), Composita (Semantics), Recursio (DSA), Bayesio (NLP), Vec Tor (Comp. Semantics / final boss), Parsemore (reserve).
decisions.rationale: Punny portmanteau naming convention adopted from user proposal. Seven professors designed with six in active use; Parsemore interchangeable with Recursio at position 4.

decisions.title: Vec Tor and Bayesio field assignments swapped
decisions.description: Vec Tor moved to Comp. Semantics final boss slot; Bayesio moved to NLP slot.
decisions.rationale: Vector semantics is a legitimate computational semantics framework; Bayesian methods are core to NLP. The swap improves thematic coherence for both professors.

decisions.title: Professor move count changed from 3 to 4
decisions.description: Each professor now has four moves. New moves added to Schwaartz (Aspiration), Composita (Deixis), Recursio (full set redesigned). Parsemore, Bayesio, Syntaxa, and Vec Tor move sets defined by user.
decisions.rationale: User proposed 4-move sets for the named professors; count standardised across the roster.

decisions.title: Player move list expanded to 6
decisions.description: Counterexample (22 dmg), Cite This! (15 dmg + skip), All-Nighter (38 dmg, 10 self), Hot Take (10 dmg, ignores debuffs), Non-Sequitur (0 dmg, halves professor next move), Correction (20 dmg, doubles to 40 if professor last move ≥30).
decisions.rationale: Six moves provide tactical variety: reliable damage, high-cost finisher, disruption, reactive counter, status clear, and soft debuff.

decisions.title: GDD split into gdd/ folder
decisions.description: game_design.md replaced with an index; content distributed across gdd/overview.md, player.md, professors.md, map.md, battle.md, dialogue.md, audio.md.
decisions.rationale: File was becoming unwieldy. Structure mirrors the tdd/ folder for consistency.

context: Resolved all remaining GDD placeholders by writing visual design notes for all seven professors — iterating through three approaches before settling on grounded-eccentric designs with one distinctive physical feature per character. Defined the full visual asset production pipeline and updated the audio spec to use per-professor battle tracks.

decisions.title: Professor visual designs finalised
decisions.description: Each professor has a written visual design note: realistic outfit, one memorable physical feature (e.g. Syntaxa — extraordinarily bushy eyebrows; Vec Tor — long patrician nose), stern gym-leader expression. Gender assigned: Schwaartz, Composita, Bayesio, Parsemore (F); Syntaxa, Recursio, Vec Tor (M).
decisions.rationale: Earlier attempts (realistic then absurdist) were either too bland or too complex for AI image generation. The final approach balances memorability with prompt-generatability.

decisions.title: Visual asset pipeline defined
decisions.description: Battle sprites: AI-generated via Midjourney at 96x96px, Schwaartz used as style anchor via --sref. Overworld sprites: hand-drawn in Aseprite at 24x24px. Sprite sheet layouts specified. Files live in assets/sprites/battle/ and assets/sprites/overworld/.
decisions.rationale: Two-tier system mirrors the Pokemon source material. Battle sprites need detail; overworld sprites need only silhouette and colour at that resolution. AI generation is unsuitable at 24x24.

decisions.title: Per-professor battle music adopted
decisions.description: Each professor has their own battle track instead of a single shared theme. Track ids follow the pattern battle_<professorId>. game.js calls audio.switchTo with the professor id on battle entry.
decisions.rationale: User sourced individual tracks for all seven professors. Per-professor music increases variety and reinforces each professor character.

title: Phaser 3 migration — TDD rewrites and battle scene milestone scoping
context: Pivoted the entire implementation from vanilla JS canvas rendering to Phaser 3. All six rendering/orchestration TDDs were rewritten to describe Phaser Scene architecture, the project charter was updated with a formal library vetting decision, and the first implementation milestone was scoped to a playable BattleScene against Prof. Schwaartz.

decisions.title: Switch from vanilla JS to Phaser 3
decisions.description: All game rendering, scene management, input, audio, and the game loop will use Phaser 3, loaded as a local vendor file.
decisions.rationale: Eliminates five categories of manual implementation; reduces complexity for a non-specialist reader given that the Phaser Scene lifecycle is well-documented and will be explained once in DESIGN.md. Preserves the no-build-step constraint.

decisions.title: Overworld sprite size changed from 24×24 to 32×32 px; tileset tile size changed from 24×24 to 16×16 px
decisions.description: Overworld character sprites are now specified at 32×32 px; tileset tiles at 16×16 px.
decisions.rationale: Most available tilesets are 16×16. The 32×32/16×16 ratio is the classic Pokemon character-to-tile proportion and significantly expands the pool of usable off-the-shelf tileset assets.

decisions.title: Tilemap implementation uses inline 2D arrays from data.js
decisions.description: OverworldScene will use this.make.tilemap({ data: region.tileMap, tileWidth: 16, tileHeight: 16 }) to build tilemaps directly from data.js arrays.
decisions.rationale: data.js already stores tile maps as 2D arrays. Phaser supports this natively, avoiding the need for external Tiled JSON files.

decisions.title: AudioScene adopted as persistent Phaser scene
decisions.description: Audio is managed by a dedicated AudioScene that runs continuously and is never stopped, ensuring music persists across scene transitions.
decisions.rationale: Phaser Sound Manager is scene-scoped; a persistent scene is the cleanest way to maintain continuous background music across overworld/battle transitions.

decisions.title: First implementation milestone scoped to BattleScene with Schwaartz
decisions.description: The first playable target is a BattleScene that boots directly, loads Schwaartz assets, and runs a complete battle with music. Prerequisite issues identified and dependency chain wired.
decisions.rationale: Battle is the most self-contained scene; it depends only on engine.js and AudioScene, not on the overworld, tilemap, or dialogue systems.

title: Battle polish — animated HP bars, multi-level sprites, engine tests, music fix
context: Extended the battle milestone with animated HP bar drains, multi-level professor sprites that unlock as HP decreases, and a sequenced turn display. Fixed three bugs discovered during testing: engine.init() never called (player HP bar unrendered), loss detection broken by engine's faint-reset, and battle music blocked by browser autoplay policy and AudioScene race condition.

decisions.title: Multi-level professor sprites via sprites[] array
decisions.description: Added a sprites[] field to professor data entries (initially Schwaartz). BattleScene loads all three textures at preload and swaps them via _updateProfSprite() as HP crosses 66% and 33% thresholds.
decisions.rationale: Gives professors visual personality that escalates with the battle; sprites already existed as _l1/_l2/_l3 files so no new assets were needed. Also resolved the existing sprite path mismatch bug.

decisions.title: HP bar drain animations via Phaser tweens
decisions.description: Replaced the flat lines[] + LOG_DISPLAY_MS approach with a _runSequence() runner that chains text steps and HP animation steps. Each step is a closure; _animateProfHP and _animatePlayerHP use tweens.addCounter to tween displayed HP each frame.
decisions.rationale: Matches the user's requested sequence: attack text → bar drains → professor attacks → bar drains. Separating draw helpers (_drawProfHPBar, _drawPlayerHPBar) from drawHPBars() was necessary to allow per-frame updates during tweens.

decisions.title: engine.init() called in OverworldScene.create()
decisions.description: engine.js exports init() but it was never called. gameState started as {}, making playerHP undefined and causing all HP bar draw calls to produce NaN.
decisions.rationale: OverworldScene is the game entry point; calling init() there ensures state is valid before any scene reads it.

decisions.title: Loss detection fixed to use raw HP value
decisions.description: engine.setPlayerHP(n) internally calls resetGame() when n<=0, restoring HP to 100. Checking engine.getState().playerHP<=0 after the call was always false. Fixed by checking the raw pre-clamp value (toHP<=0) in resolveTurn.
decisions.rationale: Side effect of the engine design; the animation refactor exposed it because HP values were being captured before each change anyway.

decisions.title: Music deferred to sound 'unlocked' event
decisions.description: BattleScene.create() now checks this.sound.locked and defers switchTo() via sound.once('unlocked',...) if true.
decisions.rationale: Browsers block audio until a user gesture. AudioScene is also loaded in parallel and may not have its tracks map populated when BattleScene.create() runs. Both issues are resolved by waiting for the unlock event, which fires on first keypress.

decisions.title: White battle background
decisions.description: Background, panel, and border colours changed to white/light grey. All text and HP bar track colours updated to dark tones for readability.
decisions.rationale: User preference.

title: BattleScene refactor, sprite integration, debug tooling
context: Refactored BattleScene.js with two targeted improvements: a centralised _setUIMode helper and a split resolveTurn into three sub-functions with a ctx object. Integrated three-level sprites for all professors, fixed pixel art rendering, and built a standalone battle debug selector page.

decisions.title: _setUIMode centralises UI visibility
decisions.description: Added _setUIMode('action'|'moves'|'log') replacing 5 scattered setVisible blocks across confirmMove, backOrFlee, selectMove, resolveTurn callback, and _showNoItems.
decisions.rationale: Removes repetition and makes UI state transitions auditable from one place without adding architectural complexity.

decisions.title: resolveTurn split into sub-functions with ctx object
decisions.description: Extracted _applyDeferredDamage(ctx), _applyPlayerMove(ctx), _applyProfessorTurn(ctx); resolveTurn becomes a thin orchestrator. Effect switch statements kept inline rather than converted to handler maps.
decisions.rationale: Reduces resolveTurn from ~150 lines to ~30; each phase is now independently readable. Handler maps rejected — charter mandates flat, explicit code readable without architectural knowledge.

decisions.title: pixelArt: true in Phaser game config
decisions.description: Added pixelArt: true to main.js Phaser config, enabling nearest-neighbour texture filtering and roundPixels.
decisions.rationale: Eliminates dark fringe artifact caused by WebGL linear filtering interpolating opaque edge pixels against transparent (black) pixels when sprites are scaled.

title: Audio system fixes and progression feature planning
context: Diagnosed and fixed three audio bugs: unregistered tracks, missing-file load errors breaking subsequent registrations, and a timing race between AudioScene initialisation and DebugSelectorScene startup. Also planned and filed eight new issues covering the experience/levelling system, defense stat, and move selection kiosk.

decisions.title: Register intro_credits and battle_default in audio.js
decisions.description: Both MP3 files existed on disk but were absent from the audioTracks array, so AudioScene never loaded them.
decisions.rationale: AudioScene only loads what is registered; missing entries cause silent no-ops in switchTo.

decisions.title: Guard AudioScene.create() with cache check
decisions.description: Added this.cache.audio.has(track.id) before each this.sound.add() call in the create loop.
decisions.rationale: Failed file loads (404s for unbuilt SFX/jingle files) do not populate the cache; calling sound.add() on an uncached key can throw and abort the loop, leaving later tracks unregistered.

decisions.title: Defer DebugSelectorScene audio until AudioScene 'create' event
decisions.description: Replaced the immediate switchTo call in DebugSelectorScene.create() with a listener on AudioScene's 'create' event.
decisions.rationale: scene.launch() queues AudioScene asynchronously; this.tracks is empty until AudioScene's own create() completes, causing switchTo to silently no-op.

decisions.title: endBattle() student path calls audio.stop() not switchTo('overworld')
decisions.description: Changed the non-professor branch of endBattle() to stop music instead of switching to 'overworld', leaving wake() responsible for starting intro_credits.
decisions.rationale: The debug selector is not the overworld. Hardcoding switchTo('overworld') meant wake() had to fight it; audio.stop() gives wake() a clean slate.

title: XP system, battle mode rename, and progression design decisions
context: Implemented the core XP and levelling system across engine.js and BattleScene.js. Made and formalised three significant design decisions: abandoning coding briefs, renaming battle debug to battle mode as a permanent feature, and establishing the session-only XP persistence model. Five issues closed.

decisions.title: Abandoned coding briefs
decisions.description: Decided not to write coding briefs for remaining modules (map, battle, dialogue, audio, game).
decisions.rationale: Briefs are a convenience copy of TDD + data.js with no canonical authority and a maintenance burden — both sources can be loaded directly during implementation.

decisions.title: Battle Debug renamed to Battle Mode
decisions.description: Renamed DebugSelectorScene to BattleModeScene, updated all references across HTML, JS, and TDD files. Promoted from debug-only tool to first-class game feature in index.html.
decisions.rationale: The selector is genuinely useful for play-testing any battle; framing it as debug undersells it and creates confusion about whether it belongs in the shipped game.

decisions.title: XP persistence model: session-only, no save/load
decisions.description: XP, level, damageBuff, defenseStat, learnedMoves, and activeMoves persist across faints within a session but reset on page reload. resetGame() does not touch progression fields.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the game simple and scope-contained; a save system would require localStorage or server state, both out of scope for a proof-of-concept.

decisions.title: XP and level-up constants defined
decisions.description: XP_PER_PROFESSOR=50, XP_PER_STUDENT=20, XP_PER_LEVEL=100, DAMAGE_BUFF_PER_LEVEL=2, DEFENSE_BUFF_PER_LEVEL=2.
decisions.rationale: Values chosen to give meaningful progression across a typical playthrough without requiring balance tuning at proof-of-concept stage.

title: Bug fixes: learned move effects, audio race condition; data layer refactor
context: Fixed two gameplay bugs (learned enemy move effects not applying, intro credits music race condition) and refactored the data layer to consolidate all move definitions into a single moves.js and normalise all dialogue into dialogue.js.

decisions.title: Extend _applyPlayerMove to handle npcMoves/professorMoves effect names
decisions.description: Added 8 new switch cases (skip_opponent, disrupt, chance_skip_opponent, self_damage, heal, chance_halve_opponent, mutual_damage_20, conditional_damage alias) plus pre-damage chance_fail and chance_bonus_10 rolls to BattleScene._applyPlayerMove.
decisions.rationale: Learned enemy moves had their effects silently ignored because the switch only handled playerMoves.js effect name strings. Root cause was that ALL_MOVE_MAP correctly resolved all move objects but the switch had no cases for npcMoves/professorMoves effect identifiers.

decisions.title: Fix audio race condition in BattleModeScene
decisions.description: Stored deferred unlock callback in this._pendingUnlock so _startBattle() can cancel it before launching BattleScene. wake() now calls audio.stop() before switchTo() to force a clean restart.
decisions.rationale: On first page load with locked audio, both BattleModeScene and BattleScene registered 'unlocked' listeners; first user gesture (clicking a battle button) fired both — race condition between intro_credits and battle music. The stop()+switchTo() fix also handles the edge case where switchTo() was a no-op due to same-track guard.

decisions.title: Consolidate all move pools into js/data/moves.js
decisions.description: Created flat moves.js exporting playerMoves, professorMoves, npcMoves. Removed professorMoves from professors.js, npcMoves from students.js, deleted playerMoves.js. Updated all imports in BattleScene.js and MoveKioskScene.js.
decisions.rationale: Move definitions were scattered across character files, requiring consumers to import from three separate character files to build ALL_MOVE_MAP. The charter explicitly names moves as a domain file in the data layer split.

decisions.title: Normalise ambient NPC dialogue into dialogue.js
decisions.description: Migrated inline string arrays from ambientNpcs.js into dialogue.js as keyed { speaker, line } sequences. ambientNpcs.js now uses dialogue: 'npc_<id>' string keys matching the battle character pattern.
decisions.rationale: Battle characters used key references into dialogue.js; ambient NPCs used inline strings — two different patterns for the same kind of data. All dialogue now lives in one predictable location.

title: Item system implementation: inventory, in-battle consumable use, effect resolution
context: Implemented the full item system: engine.js now owns inventory state and effect resolution; BattleScene.js now supports an in-battle item menu that replaces the player's move action. All four effect types (restore_hp, boost_attack, boost_defense, boost_exp) are wired. Upgrade items apply immediately on acquisition.

decisions.title: Inventory tracks {itemId, qty} entries; persists across faints
decisions.description: gameState.inventory is an array of {itemId, qty} objects. resetGame() does not clear it — items survive faints, consistent with progression-persistence policy.
decisions.rationale: Items are player progression; clearing them on faint would be punishing and inconsistent with how XP/level survive faints.

decisions.title: Upgrade items apply effect immediately on addItem(); still recorded in inventory
decisions.description: Calling addItem() with an upgrade-category item (Star Gourd, Emotional Support Pickle) triggers applyItemEffect() immediately, then records the item in inventory so hasItem() can confirm ownership.
decisions.rationale: Upgrades are permanent session buffs; applying on acquisition is simpler than deferring and avoids partial-state bugs.

decisions.title: expBoost is a one-time flat bonus consumed by the next awardXP() call
decisions.description: Consumable boost_exp items add to gameState.expBoost. awardXP() adds expBoost to the award total then resets it to 0.
decisions.rationale: Simplest correct semantics: one item use = one boosted battle. No per-battle permanent multiplier needed at this stage.

decisions.title: In-battle item use replaces the player move but opponent still acts
decisions.description: _useItemInBattle() mirrors resolveTurn() structure: deferred damage → item effect → opponent turn → return to action menu.
decisions.rationale: Using an item is a full turn action consistent with the Pokemon formula; opponent taking their turn maintains balance.

decisions.title: Item menu scoped to in-battle consumables only; overworld use deferred
decisions.description: Only items with category === 'consumable' appear in the battle item menu. Overworld item use is not implemented.
decisions.rationale: Overworld use requires separate ambient NPC and reward systems (separate open issues). Keeping scope tight.

decisions.title: Loosen flat/non-modular prohibition in project charter
decisions.description: Replaced the 'logic layer remains flat and non-modular' rule with a principle: modularise when breadth of responsibility makes a file hard to navigate; justify by reduced cognitive load; one level deep only.
decisions.rationale: BattleScene.js had grown to 1,336 lines with four distinct concerns interwoven. The flat structure was undermining the comprehensibility goal it was meant to serve.

decisions.title: Update VC-05 to test findability by name rather than single-file residence
decisions.description: VC-05 check method now reads: find the relevant file by name alone (no map required); if the mechanic spans two files, the boundary must be self-evident from their names.
decisions.rationale: Preserves the spirit of VC-05 while accommodating well-named module extractions.

decisions.rationale: Enables AudioScene to start on load so intro_credits plays immediately. Battle Mode navigates within the same Phaser instance, keeping AudioScene alive and music uninterrupted across menu and battle-selector transitions.

decisions.title: Use repeatableSource as the item reloadability flag
decisions.rationale: The existing repeatableSource field on consumables already carries the intended meaning (can this item be re-obtained). Adding a separate reloadable field would be redundant. No changes to items.js are needed.

decisions.title: Item loadout is self-maintaining — non-consumed items persist between battles
decisions.rationale: useActiveItem removes consumed items from activeItems in real time. Non-consumed items stay in the loadout automatically, so the player only re-slots what they actually used.

decisions.title: reveal_next from NPC side locks player into last-used move

decisions.title: swap_effect echoes previous effect additively, guards against recursion

title: Levelling system redesign — polynomial HP curve and HP restore on level-up
context: Redesigned the player levelling system to introduce a polynomial HP growth curve and HP restoration on level-up. The previous system had no HP gain per level and a flat XP_PER_LEVEL of 100 that left the player underlevelled relative to the final boss.

decisions.title: XP_PER_LEVEL changed from 100 to 70
decisions.rationale: 770 total XP available (21 students × 20 + 7 professors × 50). At 70 XP/level, a player who defeats every enemy earns 11 level-ups, reaching level 12.

decisions.title: HP gain formula: gain(N) = 5N
decisions.rationale: Arithmetic sequence starting at 5, 10, 15 … 55 across 11 level-ups. Total gain of 330 HP brings the player from 100 to 430 — comparable to Parsemore's 400 HP. Clean to implement as HP_GAIN_FACTOR * level.

decisions.title: HP restores fully to new max on level-up
decisions.rationale: Player HP resets to playerMaxHP (including the gain just earned) as part of the level-up event, providing a meaningful reward for levelling mid-battle.

decisions.title: playerMaxHP introduced as engine state field
decisions.rationale: Required to decouple HP ceiling from the fixed STARTING_HP constant. setPlayerHP clamp, full-restore items, and faint-respawn all now reference playerMaxHP rather than the hardcoded 100.

decisions.title: SessionStart hook guard fixed
decisions.rationale: The date +%s > .claude/session_start.tmp hook lacked the [ -d .beads ] guard, causing it to write to the wrong directory (or fail silently) when the hook fired outside the project root. Guard added to match the bd prime hook.

title: Symmetric entity model refactor
context: Refactored battleState and resolver.js from an asymmetric two-map design to a symmetric entity model. bs.player and bs.opponent now expose a unified interface; a single effects map in resolver.js handles all sides. Engine HP is synced once per turn at resolver exit rather than inside individual handlers. All hardcoded effect magnitudes moved to move-data properties.

decisions.title: Symmetric entity model with bs.player and bs.opponent
decisions.rationale: Removes the structural asymmetry that prevented professor moves from being used by the player. Both entities expose the same field interface.

decisions.title: Option A: swap pointer references (self/target) per turn
decisions.rationale: Simpler than copy-merge; applyPlayerMove binds self=player/target=opponent, applyOpponentTurn swaps them. No reconciliation step needed beyond the single engine sync.

decisions.title: Engine HP sync once per turn at resolver exit
decisions.rationale: Avoids multiple setPlayerHP calls mid-handler; handlers mutate entity.hp directly. Items used between turns are picked up by a sync-from-engine at the start of each resolver call.

decisions.title: Generic move magnitudes — no hardcoded numbers in effect IDs
decisions.rationale: Effect IDs like skip_self_2 and self_vuln_next encoded magnitude in the name. Replaced with skip_self(skipTurns), self_vuln(vulnTurns, vulnBonus), boost_sustained(boostTurns, boostAmount), etc.

decisions.title: reveal_next kept intentionally asymmetric
decisions.rationale: Player reveal pre-rolls a random opponent move; opponent reveal locks the player into their currently selected move. These are different mechanics; unifying them would require coupling to move-pool access patterns that differ by side. Documented in resolver.js.

title: Resolver effects audit and move pool consolidation
context: Full audit of all effects in resolver.js against moves.js. Found and fixed three bugs, redesigned the priority mechanic, and merged playerMoves into npcMoves.

decisions.title: disrupt replaced with chance_skip_opponent
decisions.rationale: big_o and dep_parse were silently broken — no handler existed for disrupt. Initially fixed as deterministic halve, then changed to chance_skip_opponent (skipChance 0.5) on the principle that no roster move should guarantee a skip or disrupt.

decisions.title: self_vuln player-side fix
decisions.rationale: player.vulnTurns was set by impostor_syndrome but never consumed in applyOpponentTurn. The effect did nothing for the player. Fixed by adding the consumption check after the defenseStat deduction.

decisions.title: cancel_effect implemented for next_token_prediction
decisions.rationale: New symmetric predict-and-cancel mechanic. Randomly guesses one of the opponent's moves and stores it as guessedMove. On the following turn, if the actual move matches, it is cancelled before damage or effect fires. Works both ways — NPC using it predicts and can cancel the player's move.

decisions.title: priority effect redesigned with outgoingBonus fallback
decisions.rationale: Player priority was a no-op in normal play — player already goes first by default, so setting player.priority changed nothing unless the opponent also had priority. New logic in BattleScene: if player.priority && !opponent.priority, award +10 outgoingBonus on the following move. hot_take description updated.

decisions.title: playerMoves merged into npcMoves
decisions.rationale: playerMoves were partial duplicates of npcMoves entries. Player stats win on conflicts: hot_take keeps priority effect (NPC had clear_debuff), undergrad_flashback keeps damage 11 (NPC had 14). Single pool is simpler to browse and eliminates the implicit overwrite behaviour that ALL_MOVE_MAP was relying on.

decisions.rationale: Self-documenting: the controls reference is inside the menu it documents; tab width recalculated from ÷3 (133px) to ÷4 (100px)

decisions.title: Back as a menu entry in BattleScene submenus
decisions.rationale: Consistent with the unified control schema — Enter/Space confirms Back, ESC remains a parallel shortcut

decisions.title: Strip BattleModeScene to opponent-picker only
decisions.rationale: Moves/Items tabs were redundant with KioskScene (accessible via I from anywhere); removing them simplifies the scene and eliminates a parallel loadout path

decisions.title: Keyboard navigation for MainMenuScene and BattleModeScene
decisions.rationale: Consistency with the established control schema; ↑↓ navigate, Enter/Space confirm, ESC back, ←→ page in battle mode

decisions.title: Fix input.keyboard.enabled instead of input.enabled
decisions.rationale: Phaser 3: input.enabled only disables pointer/mouse events; keyboard plugin has its own enabled flag that must be set separately to gate key events

resolved_issues.title: Keyboard nav: MainMenuScene + simplified BattleModeScene
resolved_issues.resolution: Implemented keyboard nav on both scenes; BattleModeScene tabs removed; Phaser keyboard input bug fixed
